h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I keep trying to make that 4 count beats too
scored.statedInsightEvidence: So 3/8 is three eighth-note beats, not a fourth count.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to confuse the bottom number with a count of beats or a speed mark.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Three marks in this bar, lower box takes 8 from the card.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the new rule to draw the correct notes and identifies the correct lower number.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical covering mechanism to prevent the learner's cognitive error of adding a fourth tile.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: the card changes the lower number and the beat mark drawn under each tile; it adds no fourth beat.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly answers the learner's specific question about the swapped card's role without changing the overall framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: the card changes the lower number and the beat mark drawn under each tile; it adds no fourth beat.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly answers the learner's specific question about the swapped card's role without changing the overall framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical covering mechanism to prevent the learner's cognitive error of adding a fourth tile.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical covering mechanism to prevent the learner's cognitive error of adding a fourth tile.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner systematically applies the newly introduced rule to each arrow on the diagram.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The up arrow is track on cart. Track card goes there; the track pushes up on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently identifies the correct interacting object and direction for a new arrow, demonstrating mastery of the enacted criterion.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a reversal in the tutor's strategy.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: The up arrow is track on cart. Track card goes there; the track pushes up on the cart.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently identifies the correct interacting object and direction for a new arrow, demonstrating mastery of the enacted criterion.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a reversal in the tutor's strategy.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a reversal in the tutor's strategy.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still keep wanting `rainfall_mm` to stand for the whole rainfall column.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will smoothly apply the established row-limiting pattern to the next column.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: For `month`: recorded month for that city-month row. Testing `April`, it should be one row's month, not a statement about the whole `month` column
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently applies the limiting pattern to a new field and tests it against a specific value.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The transcript ends before the tutor can respond to the learner's doubt.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no subsequent tutor turn to adapt to the learner's revised framing.
